item.title: Potential agreement with Iran would include opening of Strait of Hormuz, Trump says
item.description: Hours after saying the U.S. would attack civilian infrastructure and even take over Iran's energy facilities, President Trump announced that a deal could be signed over the weekend and that the documents are in "pretty final shape." Caitlin Huey Burns reports on the potential deal to strike a deal.

item.title: What it was like inside Madison Square Garden during the Knicks' historic Game 4 comeback
item.description: CBS News executive producer Alvin Patrick was in attendance with his son as the New York Knicks pulled off the biggest comeback in NBA Finals history Thursday night, beating the San Antonio Spurs 107-106 to take a 3-1 series lead. Patrick joined Lindsey Reiser to recount the night.

item.title: She confided in ChatGPT the night of her suicide. Now, her mother is suing OpenAI.
item.description: A mother has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging the chatbot's design led to her daughter's suicide.

item.title: Retired USWNT midfielder Lori Lindsey previews FIFA Men's World Cup
item.description: The 2026 FIFA Men's World Cup is officially underway, with the first match kicking off on Thursday between Mexico and South Africa. Lori Lindsey, retired midfielder for the U.S. Women's National Soccer Team and "MLS on Apple TV" analyst, joins to discuss.

item.title: Thune under pressure from Trump to fire Senate parliamentarian over voter ID bill
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune is pushing back against President Trump's call to fire the Senate parliamentarian. Daniella Diaz, politics reporter for NOTUS, joins to explain. Then, Democratic strategist John McCarthy and former GOP Rep. Peter Meijer of Michigan join with analysis.

item.title: World Cup kicks off in Mexico with Shakira and a home team victory
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup opening ceremony festivities got underway a little over an hour before the first kickoff in Mexico City's Estadio Azteca.

item.title: China, Russia, Iran and North Korea are undermining global order and free societies, report finds
item.description: A new report from the George W. Bush Institute report is raising red flags about the threat that authoritarian regimes pose to human rights and democracy around the world, singling out China, Russia, Iran and North Korea. The report's authors, Igor Khrestin and Joseph Kim, join "The Takeout" to unpack the findings.

item.title: U.S.-Iran memo of understanding likely to be signed next week, sources say
item.description: The signing of the memorandum or letter of intent would kick off 60 days of talks to negotiate details of an enduring U.S.-Iran agreement.

item.title: Markwayne Mullin defends decision to deny U.S. entry to Somali World Cup referee
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary MarkWayne Mullin is defending a decision to deny a World Cup referee from Somalia entry into the U.S. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports on that and Skyler Henry reports on security efforts surrounding the World Cup.

item.title: Vance Boelter changes federal plea to guilty in lawmaker shootings
item.description: Vance Boelter pleaded guilty to the shootings of two Minnesota lawmakers and their spouses on Thursday as part of an agreement with federal prosecutors.

item.title: Trump taps prosecutor Jay Clayton as next director of national intelligence
item.description: Jay Clayton is currently the U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of New York.

item.title: Trump says "settlement" reached on Iran, signing could happen this weekend
item.description: President Trump said earlier Thursday he called off new military strikes on Iran, hours after threatening to escalate the war.

item.title: Rep. Kevin Kiley calls for congressional involvement in deal to end Iran war
item.description: President Trump abruptly canceled planned air strikes on Iran just hours before the scheduled launch on Thursday, citing progress in peace negotiations. Independent Rep. Kevin Kiley of California joins "The Takeout" with his reaction.

item.title: As fans from around the world gather to watch the World Cup, doctor shares tips on staying healthy
item.description: Over the next month, fans will be packing stadiums and other venues to watch the World Cup. Dr. Abraar Karan, an infectious diseases expert at Stanford University, joins CBS News with tips for staying safe and healthy.

item.title: U.S. and Iran expected to have 60 days to reach deal upon letter of intent signing
item.description: Hours before planned strikes against Iran on Thursday, President Trump announced the attacks were off, and said a peace agreement had been reached with Tehran. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns, Eleanor Watson and Aaron MacLean have the latest.

item.title: Who is Jay Clayton, Trump's pick for director of national intelligence?
item.description: President Trump is nominating former Securities and Exchange Commission Chairman Jay Clayton to be the next director of national intelligence. CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Expert says there's "no question" U.S. could take Kharg Island, just a matter of cost
item.description: President Trump announced Thursday that he called off scheduled strikes on Iran, citing progress on a potential deal with the country. Jon Alterman, Brzezinski Chair in Global Security and Geostrategy at CSIS, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (June 14)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup kicks off with 3 opening ceremonies
item.description: With matches being played in 11 cities across the U.S., Mexico and Canada​, fans are getting three World Cup opening ceremonies.

item.title: When will the Iran war end? Here's what Trump has said.
item.description: President Trump has offered timelines of days and weeks for the Iran war, but a solution remains elusive.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: World Cup kicks off with match in Mexico
item.description: The Men's World Cup officially kicked off Thursday with a match between Mexico and South Africa in Mexico City. Billions of fans are expected to watch the tournament worldwide. CBS News correspondent Nicole Valdes reports on fans' excitement.

item.title: Relative of teen charged in Anna Kepner killing says Kepner's father, stepmother should face charges
item.description: The step-grandmother of Timothy Hudson, the 16-year-old stepbrother of Anna Kepner, who has been charged in her death and sexual assault, says Kepner's father and stepmother should be charged with parental neglect.

item.title: Inmate's nitrogen gas execution tonight hinges on last-minute appeal
item.description: The Alabama Attorney General's Office petitioned the Supreme Court to reverse a federal judge's decision to permanently ban the state from putting Jeffrey Lee to death using nitrogen hypoxia.

item.title: The World Cup will draw millions of fans, but economic payoff is modest
item.description: Although the five-week soccer tournament starting on Thursday is the largest sporting event ever, the U.S. economic gains are likely to be muted.

item.title: U.S. monitoring Chinese activity in South China Sea around disputed shoal
item.description: Analysts are scrutinizing recent Chinese maritime operations near Scarborough Shoal, an uninhabited but strategically located atoll near the Philippine island of Luzon, U.S. officials said.

item.title: U.S. cruise passengers accused of assaulting police in Bahamas
item.description: Four police officers were injured, including one who was taken to the hospital, authorities said.

item.title: See when the U.S. men's soccer team is playing in 2026 World Cup
item.description: The U.S. men's national soccer team, which last appeared at the 2022 World Cup, will face Paraguay to kick off its 2026 World Cup.

item.title: House rejects last-minute extension for key spy power amid Pulte uproar
item.description: The House on Thursday defeated a last-ditch effort to extend a key spy authority until early July.

item.title: Human remains found in sleeping bag in national park identified 26 years later
item.description: Forensic tests helped identify a man whose remains were found inside a sleeping bag in Washington state in 2000.
